title: IREN Drops 14% as Bitcoin Mining Revenue Falls to $167 Million
text:
Iren (IREN) experienced a significant stock decline after disappointing Q2 earnings results. The company's total revenue came in at $184.7 million, falling substantially short of the $229.65 million consensus estimate by approximately 20%.

The primary driver of investor concern was the collapse in mining operations. As the company redirects resources toward artificial intelligence infrastructure, Bitcoin mining revenue fell to $167.4 million in Q2, down from $232.9 million in Q1. This deterioration coincided with broader cryptocurrency weakness, as Bitcoin declined roughly 32% from its mid-January peak.

Iren is undergoing a strategic transformation toward AI cloud services, backed by a $9.7 billion Microsoft contract. However, market skepticism remains high. The company's fledgling AI Cloud division generated only $7.3 million in Q1 revenue—far below the billions projected. Despite these headwinds, Wall Street maintains confidence, with 11 of 14 analysts maintaining Buy ratings and a consensus price target of $79.
